metadata.title:
Officers Uncover Hiding Place Of


Train Robbers at Trent.

pages.raw_text:
his revolver as warning to others
of the party. Three men arose
from the bushes near him and
started to run. Draper fired two
shots at them, but fear of wounding
some of his own party compelled
him to withhold any more accurate
shots. One of the three men stop
ped at the first shot and held up
his hands. He was quickly hand
cuffed. BY this time others of the
party ran up and after turning the
captive over to Deputy Long the
rest of the posse set off in the di-
rection taken by the other two men.


Long, the prisoner and Tom
Smith returned to town this morn
ing. The man gave the name of
H. d. Murphy. He is only boy,
scarcely Of age, five feet seven
inches in height and weighs about
$50 pounds. On his right arm is
tattooed the bust of a woman and
"W. W." is tattooed on the left
forearm. He denies that he and
his friends were preparing to hold
up the overland and says they were
camped near the bridge to wait for
a freight train to bring them to
town. He is being kept in solitary
confinement and later today will be
sweated in hopes that he will con-
fess.


The evidence duplicating Muir
ply is given by the station agent
at Trent, who identified his descrip-
ton as that of a man who came
into the Office few days ago and
made inquiry about the trains.
When he left the agent noted the
disappearance of quantity of fuse
that had been kept there and was
certain his visitor took it.


BY the merest chance the at
tempted holdup was foiled. During
the past week three attempts have
been made near Trent to flag the
overland. Saturday the supposed
bandits stopped freight by mis-
take This has caused suspicion
that all was not right and when
last night another attempt to flag
the train was made Engineer
Fields threw the throttle wide open
and sped past man who was way
ing red light as a danger signal.
On the arrival of the train here
Special Agent Joel Hindman was
notified. He decided that there was
possible chance to capture the
men and hurriedly called together
the posse,


In searching for the two men who
escaped last night the officers
stopped the first inbound Occur
d'Alene train ON the Inland. They
searched two men who got on at
Greenacres but were satisfied of
their innocence.


After daylight this morning the
posse trailed the other two suspects
as far as the Spokane & Inland
electric line. 1t is now believed they
boarded a train and came to town.
Local police have been furnished
with their descriptions. Tie posse
has returned to town. Harry
Draper says he believes he hit one
of the men. as he stumbled when
shot at but quickly recovered.
